page: Microsoft Azure E4-2ads_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EADSv5 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. It features 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. Additionally, the server includes 154 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, though it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% in stress-ng testing, while single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show average performance. However, multi-core CPU performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng and the bottom 10% for Geekbench multi-core testing. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with small-block read-only bandwidth scoring in the bottom 10% and large-block read/write bandwidth in the bottom 25%. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also fall into the bottom 10% performance tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E4-2ads_v5 offers a high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive parallel processing. The inclusion of 154 GB of local storage provides a dedicated space for temporary files or caching. Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and the modest multi-core performance, this server is best suited for light database caching, memory-intensive single-threaded applications, small-scale enterprise applications, and workloads requiring high RAM capacity without heavy computational demands.
description: Microsoft Azure E4-2ads_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the EADSv5 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs across 1 physical core, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 154 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. While single-core CPU performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng benchmarks, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. This server offers high memory density relative to its compute footprint, making it a cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand high parallel processing power. It is best suited for memory-bound applications, light database caching, and single-threaded tasks requiring a substantial memory footprint.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4-2ads_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, 2 vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and 154 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM density and local storage for memory-bound, single-threaded enterprise workloads.

32.0 GB of RAM providing a high 16.0 GB per core ratio
